evals.prompt: How do I deploy the latest code to my Raspberry Pi? I've just finished fixing a bug in the tplink_switch module and want to get it onto the Pi.
evals.expected_output: Clear step-by-step instructions covering: run tests first, then use deploy-to-pi.sh, and verify with connect-pi.sh --check. Should include the correct WSL paths and any relevant flags.

evals.prompt: I want to add a Tuya temperature sensor to the project. Where should I put the code and how should I structure it?
evals.expected_output: Guidance following project conventions: create src/python/tuya.py with an isolated interface class, add to configs/devices.example.yaml, write tests in tests/python/test_tuya.py, wire into controller.py. Should mention TUYA_ACCESS_ID env var and local-network preference.

evals.prompt: My kitchen switch isn't responding. How do I debug it?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step debug process: run discover_tplink_switches.py to check LAN visibility, then test direct control with tplink_switch --host <IP> status. Should include correct command syntax and mention checking TPLINK_USERNAME/PASSWORD for newer devices.
